REFERENCE: This citation contains multiple serious red flags indicating it is likely fabricated or heavily corrupted.
authors: The first author 'Dewhurst, P.R.' does not match any known major author of a remdesivir outpatient trial. The well-known study on early remdesivir in outpatients is by Gottlieb RL et al., published in the New England Journal of Medicine.
title: The title 'Early Remdesivir to Prevent Progression to Severe Covid-19 in Outpatients' closely matches the title of the Gottlieb et al. (2022) paper in NEJM, but the citation attributes it to the wrong authors, journal, DOI, and possibly date.
journal_name: The paper described — 'Early Remdesivir to Prevent Progression to Severe Covid-19 in Outpatients' — was published in the New England Journal of Medicine (NEJM) by Gottlieb et al. (2022), not in Science.
journal_volume: Volume 373 of Science corresponds to 2021, which is consistent with the year given but does not validate the citation given the other errors.
journal_issue: While Science does publish issues in the 373 range around 2021, and 6562 is a plausible issue number, the volume/issue pairing cannot be verified as correct given the DOI and author mismatches.
journal_page: The page range 1026-1069 is suspiciously wide for a single article and does not correspond to any known remdesivir outpatient study.
elocator: The page range '1026-1069' spans 44 pages, which is extraordinarily long for a single research article in Science or any major journal.
publication_date: The date '2021 Sep 24' uses an inconsistent format (might be meant as September 24, 2021). It does not match the publication date of the actual remdesivir outpatient study (February 16, 2022 in NEJM).
doi: The DOI '10.1038/science.abj3321' uses the prefix 10.1038, which is assigned to Nature Publishing Group. Science magazine is published by AAAS, whose DOI prefix is 10.1126. This is a fundamental mismatch — a Science paper would never carry a 10.1038 DOI.
pmcid: No PubMed Central ID is provided in this citation.
pmid: No PubMed ID is provided in this citation, which is a limitation but not an error per se.
